opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I had a simple cold three weeks ago, but now my body feels like it weighs a thousand pounds, and taking a shower puts me in bed for two days.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I've been floored by exhaustion for weeks after a cold and can barely function — I want to know what's wrong.

symptom_narrative.onset: 3 weeks ago after an uncomplicated cold
symptom_narrative.character: profound exhaustion, unrefreshing sleep, post-exertional crashes, brain fog, mild aches
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: body feels like it weighs a thousand pounds; a shower puts me in bed for 2 days
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: mild aches, brain fog, intermittent feeling feverish (no measured fever); NO chest pain, no breathlessness at rest, no palpitations, no weight loss, no drenching night sweats, no focal weakness
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: largely bed-bound, struggling with basic tasks

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; severely functionally impaired; anxious about a permanent condition

objective_data_offered.value: profound fatigue with post-exertional crashes; speaking slowly, chronically fatigued not acutely toxic
no chest pain, no breathlessness at rest, no palpitations at rest
